title: Phase 3: Tutorial Mode, New Enemy Types, and Progressive Levels

requirements.text: Add a step-by-step Tutorial Mode to the F-19 Flight Simulator that walks players through each core mechanic in sequence: basic movement (W/A/S/D or equivalent), throttle control, shooting enemies, locking on with targeting, dodging incoming missiles, and deploying flares as countermeasures. Each step should display a clear on-screen prompt/overlay describing the action required and only advance to the next step once the player successfully performs it.

quotes: Step-by-step guided walkthrough covering all mechanics: movement, shooting, dodging missiles, deploying flares

acceptanceCriteria: Tutorial mode is accessible from the main menu or game start screen
Steps cover: movement, throttle, shooting, lock-on targeting, missile dodge, and flare deployment
Each step shows a clear on-screen instruction prompt
The tutorial only advances to the next step after the player successfully performs the required action
A progress indicator shows which tutorial step the player is on
Tutorial can be exited/skipped at any point
Completing the tutorial transitions the player to the first progressive level

requirements.text: Implement four new enemy types in the F-19 Flight Simulator, each with distinct behavior and visual appearance: (1) Fast Jets — high-speed aircraft that are difficult to track and require lead-aiming; (2) Armored Enemies — enemies that require multiple hits to destroy, with a visible health bar showing remaining HP; (3) Missile-Launching Drones — enemies that fire homing missiles at the player, requiring flare countermeasures to evade; (4) Ground-to-Air Turrets — static ground-based enemies that fire rapid projectiles at low-altitude aircraft. Each type should have a distinct 3D geometry/appearance and award different coin/score values on destruction.

quotes: varied set that fits the game
Fast jets (quick, hard to track), Armored enemies (require multiple hits), Missile-launching drones (adds flare-counter gameplay), Ground-to-air turrets (static but deadly)

acceptanceCriteria: Fast Jets spawn and move at significantly higher speed than standard enemies and are visually distinct
Armored enemies display a health bar and require at least 3 hits to destroy
Missile-launching drones fire homing missiles that can be countered by deploying flares
Ground-to-air turrets are placed on terrain and fire projectiles upward at the player when within range
Each enemy type has a unique 3D model/geometry distinguishable from others
Each enemy type awards a different coin and score value on destruction
All new enemy types integrate with the existing targeting/lock-on system in useF19FlightSimulatorGame.ts

requirements.text: Add a Progressive Levels system to the F-19 Flight Simulator with at least 6 levels of escalating difficulty. Each level should combine multiple win conditions chosen from: survive for X seconds, destroy X enemies, reach a score threshold, and protect an objective (e.g., a carrier or ground base). Difficulty should escalate across levels by increasing enemy count, enemy speed, missile frequency, and introducing new enemy types from REQ-2 at later levels. Each level should display its objectives clearly at the start and track progress in the HUD during play. A level-complete screen should show results and allow progression to the next level.

quotes: levels can combine multiple conditions at once
Escalating difficulty: more enemies, faster missiles, tighter time limits
Combined win conditions per level (e.g., survive 60s AND destroy 10 enemies AND reach 500 points)
A protect-the-objective mission type mixed in

acceptanceCriteria: At least 6 progressive levels are defined with increasing difficulty parameters
Each level specifies a combination of at least 2 win conditions from: survive X seconds, destroy X enemies, reach score threshold, protect objective
Level objectives are displayed in an overlay at the start of each level
HUD shows live progress toward all active objectives during gameplay
Enemies become more numerous, faster, and include new enemy types in higher levels
A protect-the-objective mechanic exists where a target (e.g., carrier) has HP and the level fails if it is destroyed
A level-complete screen shows score, coins earned, and objectives met, with a button to proceed to the next level
A level-fail screen appears when the player crashes or fails to meet a time-limited objective, with a retry button
Levels are unlocked sequentially; completing a level unlocks the next

requirements.text: Add a Level Select / Mode Select screen to the F-19 Flight Simulator that lets players choose between Tutorial Mode (REQ-1) and Progressive Levels (REQ-3). The screen should show each level's name, difficulty rating, and locked/unlocked status. Completed levels should display the player's best score.
acceptanceCriteria: A mode/level select screen is shown when starting the F-19 game instead of going directly into freeplay
Tutorial Mode option is always unlocked and accessible
Progressive levels are listed with name, difficulty indicator, and locked/unlocked status
Locked levels show a lock icon; unlocked levels are clickable
Completed levels display the player's best score beneath their entry
A 'Freeplay' option to play the existing sandbox mode is also available

constraints: All new game logic must be added to or extend the existing useF19FlightSimulatorGame.ts hook and F19FlightSimulatorGame.tsx page — do not create a separate game hook
New enemy types must integrate with the existing targeting, missile, and flare systems in F19EnemyTargets.tsx and F19Missiles.tsx
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
All level progress and unlock state must be persisted to localStorage
nonGoals: Backend leaderboard for level scores (localStorage only for now)
New aircraft models or player aircraft changes
Changes to the coin shop pricing or existing purchasable items
Changes to existing freeplay/sandbox game mechanics
